Commercial display rule: The report shows full modeled operational savings and an illustrative 35% Glyph Systems cut. The cut is a planning illustration, not a contractual entitlement. Customer-retained value, audit rules, delivery costs, and the final performance percentage require written agreement and real-data validation.
Evidence boundary: This is a controlled matched recovery benchmark for a medium-volume financial processor. Modeled savings represent avoided operational friction and waste, not guaranteed liquid cash, realized customer savings, or Glyph Systems revenue. No market prediction, trading, fee selection, account access, or electrical-energy claim is included.

Executive result

Logic Engine and the industry best-practice baseline processed the same transactions, service outcomes, physical capacity, queue limits, and incident schedule across 72 complete independent ten-minute rounds. The cumulative full modeled operational savings were $5,729.77, equal to $7.9580 per minute. The illustrative Glyph Systems cut was $2,005.42, equal to $2.7853 per minute.

The complete-round distribution had a median of $3.1991/min, a 10th percentile of $0.4552/min, and a worst round of $-0.0442/min.

Methodology and fairness

Each complete round lasts ten minutes: twenty seconds of clean stable-state calibration, an incident severity derived from an official public status record, matched transaction-level disruption and recovery, and a one-minute final clean drain. The first 72 incident records, mapped profiles, and deterministic round seeds are cryptographically precommitted before results are observed.

All five controls receive identical transactions, hidden service outcomes, incident schedule, physical service ceiling, queue limit, retry limit, idempotency capability, visible state source, restoration timing, and frozen cost coefficients. Controls differ only in scheduling, holding, verification, retry timing, isolation, retained-state use, and recovery-policy interpretation.

Program 40 uses 50 TPS baseline demand, 90 TPS clean capacity, and a 15,000-transaction queue. Cost assumptions are frozen at 35% of the Program 35 large-system profile. Public GitHub Status and Cloudflare Status records determine incident severity and ordering input; the program does not claim to reproduce either provider’s private traffic or losses. Controllers cannot access the accounting model.

The adaptive-state track carries only clean stable-state estimates between rounds. Transactions, queues, active incident state, accumulated system pressure, unresolved recovery load, recovery-hold state, and staged-release state reset every round.

Public incident records SHA-256: 0e34aaa64925d7c7b66f19a4274386035c5a5d8287cf578f5081e7ea62b806b3. Source mode: LIVE_FETCH. First-72 incident/profile/seed commitment: c7c9e45db5a6f6fb62a8e4eebe8981ce3b3e2269abef8bb780b8b1f9bfce2a20.
